you're missing what I said.

Change line 72 to use " " (instead of "")

then change this if (and others) to have spaces on both sides and eliminate half of your tests:

if (!(commands.contains(" -i ") || commands.commands.contains(" -_component_spec "))

eliminating half of the calls to contains()

according to 
https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-cli/apidocs/org/apache/commons/cli/Options.html

it says:

public Option getOption​(String opt)
Retrieve the Option matching the long or short name specified.
The leading hyphens in the name are ignored (up to 2).


So if the longOpt argument were "blueprint_path" instead of "_blueprint_path", it should have been accepted with the original argument list.

It's been some time since I used that API, but I would be really surprised if it got broken.

Please write a JIRA to revisit this. All this copying of the options should be totally unneeded.

if (commands.contains(" -n ") || commands.contains(" -_blueprint_name "))

Consider printing some instructions when the jar is called in a wrong way.
Double quote shoudn't be here probably
Current name is onap-blueprint-generator I think.
-o flag is for service name override (not to specify "OnapBlueprint"). There is no argument for service name override so command should probably fail.
I believe that cobertura is not entirely compatibile with java 11. It was addressed here: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/dcaegen2/platform/+/112818
Shouldn't onapCommonUtils.printInstructions() be an else for the outer if?
Not sure about the coverage paths (but i guess it could be fixed later).

Consider moving description higher (preferably before imports).

When mapping to yaml the order of variables is retained.

Null check is needed here. blueprintName in Input class is set to null when there is no -n option provided in run command. (additionally blueprintName is defined as optional in printInstructions)

If you are going to change anything here please also format the code - it's hard to read because everything is flattened.

I think that Boolean should be used, so when the value is not explicitly set (useExisting is null), then there is no entry in resulting blueprint.
Now it defaults to false.
